title: Rate of change
short: How fast a function's output changes — a slope.
full:
The rate of change of a function measures how quickly its output rises or falls. The average rate of change over an interval [a, b] is the slope of the secant line joining the two endpoints: (f(b) − f(a))/(b − a). The instantaneous rate of change at a single point is the slope of the tangent line there — the value of the derivative f '(a).

The instantaneous rate is the limit of the average rate as the interval shrinks to a point, which is exactly how the derivative is defined.
